name: Sierra Leone (Republic of Sierra Leone)
summary: Sierra Leone is a West African mixed-legal-system republic combining English common-law substantive heritage (via colonial inheritance) with customary-law personal-status jurisdiction operating through Local Courts under the Local Courts Act 2011. Family-law framework operates under the Child Rights Act 2007, the Christian Marriage Act, the Mohammedan Marriage Act, the Civil Marriage Act, the Customary Marriage and Divorce Act 2009, and the Devolution of Estates Act 2007. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by Child Rights Act Part VI. The Supreme Court of Sierra Leone is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the High Court (Family Division), Magistrates' Courts, and Local Courts for customary-law matters. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health and Sanitation framework. Sierra Leone is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the best-interests-of-the-child principle codified in Child Rights Act s. 1. Sierra Leone is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Child Rights Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Child Rights Act codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 1), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions.

statutory_framework.title: Customary Marriage and Divorce Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute on customary marriage and divorce.

statutory_framework.title: Local Courts Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute establishing Local Courts for customary-law jurisdiction.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Sierra Leone
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health and Sanitation, Sierra Leone
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Sierra Leonean family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Province of Freedom + Sierra-Leone-Company + freed-slave-settlement framework
key_developments.description: Province of Freedom established 22 May 1787 by Sierra Leone Company under abolitionist Granville Sharp — substantively distinctive globally British-abolitionist-freed-slave-settlement-on-African-coast framework. Foundational pre-1808-Crown-Colony framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and West African common-law substantive heritage.

key_developments.title: Sierra Leone Crown Colony + British-administration framework
key_developments.description: Sierra Leone Crown Colony established 1 January 1808 with Freetown as capital — substantively distinctive West African British-Crown-Colony framework. Subsequent 1896 British Protectorate over Sierra Leone hinterland. Foundational pre-1961-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Independence + Westminster-framework + Commonwealth-realm framework
key_developments.description: Sierra Leone achieved independence from the United Kingdom 27 April 1961 — substantively distinctive West African Westminster-framework-+-Commonwealth-realm framework with Queen Elizabeth II as initial Queen of Sierra Leone. Foundational pre-1971-republic-transition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Republic transition + Stevens-presidency + APC-one-party-state framework
key_developments.description: Sierra Leone became a republic 19 April 1971 under Siaka Stevens presidency — substantively distinctive West African Commonwealth-republic-+-APC-one-party-state framework. Foundational pre-1991-2002-Civil-War framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Sierra Leone ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + UNCRC-applicability framework
key_developments.description: Sierra Leone ratified UNCRC 18 June 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2007 Child Rights Act substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Sierra Leone Civil War + RUF-+-Foday-Sankoh-+-blood-diamonds framework
key_developments.description: Sierra Leone Civil War 23 March 1991-18 January 2002 — substantively distinctive West African post-Cold-War-civil-war framework. RUF (Revolutionary United Front) under Foday Sankoh with Charles Taylor backing. Estimated 50,000-300,000 deaths + 2.6 million displaced substantively reshaping demographic framework. Substantive blood-diamonds framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and Special Court for Sierra Leone establishment 2002.

key_developments.title: Child Rights Act + Devolution-of-Estates-Act framework
key_developments.description: Federal Child Rights Act 2007 enacted codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 1), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions. Devolution of Estates Act 2007 codifying inheritance provisions. Substantively significant West African child-rights-+-inheritance-modernisation framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Customary Marriage and Divorce Act + multi-track-marriage-framework consolidation
key_developments.description: Customary Marriage and Divorce Act 2009 enacted establishing federal statute on customary marriage and divorce — substantively significant West African multi-track-marriage-framework consolidation (Christian + Mohammedan + Civil + Customary). Substantive family-law-implementation framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Local Courts Act + customary-law-jurisdiction framework
key_developments.description: Local Courts Act 2011 enacted establishing Local Courts for customary-law jurisdiction — substantively significant West African customary-court framework. Foundational substantive customary-court framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Bio re-election + HRC-SL + GEWE Act 2022 framework
key_developments.description: Julius Maada Bio re-elected 27 June 2023 substantively significant continuing-presidency framework. Human Rights Commission of Sierra Leone (HRC-SL) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Child Rights Act 2007 + Customary Marriage and Divorce Act 2009 framework. Gender Equality and Women's Empowerment (GEWE) Act 2022 substantively significant for women's-rights framework affecting family-law adjudication. Substantively significant West African institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of Sierra Leone + Local Courts — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + Bio-presidency framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of Sierra Leone and Local Courts continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Child Rights Act 2007 s. 1 + Customary Marriage and Divorce Act 2009 + Local Courts Act 2011 framework in custody disputes within Julius Maada Bio (SLPP) presidency since 4 April 2018 (re-elected 27 June 2023). Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Sierra Leone operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — English common-law substantive heritage + Christian/Mohammedan/Civil/Customary marriage frameworks operating in parallel. Within the West African common-law cluster with Nigeria, Ghana, Gambia.
Multi-track marriage framework (Christian + Mohammedan + Civil + Customary) is structurally distinctive.
Non-Hague Convention status places Sierra Leone in the non-Hague West African cluster.
editorial_notes: Sierra Leone jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Province-of-Freedom-1787-to-Bio-presidency trajectory: 1787-Province-of-Freedom-+-Sierra-Leone-Company + 1808-Sierra-Leone-Crown-Colony + 1961-independence-from-UK + 1971-republic-transition-+-Stevens-presidency + 1990-Sierra-Leone-UNCRC-ratification + 1991-Sierra-Leone-Civil-War-+-RUF + 2007-Child-Rights-Act-+-Devolution-of-Estates-Act + 2009-Customary-Marriage-and-Divorce-Act + 2011-Local-Courts-Act + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Local-Courts-+-Bio-presidency.

Mixed-legal-system West Africa (English common-law substantive + multi-track marriage frameworks (Christian + Mohammedan + Civil + Customary) + customary-law via Local Courts). Child Rights Act 2007 + Customary Marriage Act 2009 + Local Courts Act 2011 + non-Hague Convention. Province of Freedom 1787 abolitionist-freed-slave-settlement globally distinctive.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Child Rights Act 2007 s. 1 + Customary Marriage and Divorce Act 2009 + Local Courts Act 2011 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins West-African + common-law + multi-track-marriage-Christian-+-Mohammedan-+-Civil-+-Customary + Province-of-Freedom-1787-Sierra-Leone-Company-+-Granville-Sharp-abolitionist-globally-distinctive + Sierra-Leone-Crown-Colony-1808-+-British-Protectorate-1896 + independence-from-UK-1961 + republic-transition-1971-+-Stevens-presidency + Sierra-Leone-Civil-War-1991-2002-RUF-+-Foday-Sankoh-+-blood-diamonds-+-50000-300000-deaths + Special-Court-for-Sierra-Leone-2002 + Child-Rights-Act-2007 + Customary-Marriage-and-Divorce-Act-2009 + Local-Courts-Act-2011 + Bio-SLPP-2018-2024-presidency + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
